Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "BrightLoop" or "Series B" or "Tessa Moreno" (or similar) to find Tessa's expansion request and Mina's prior communications. Expected findings: Tessa Moreno's email (sender: tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com) announcing BrightLoop's Series B close and requesting six new May relocations to Boston: (1) Oliver Bancroft, London to Boston, UK-to-US transfer needing visa support and customs/shipping help; (2) Priya Naidu, Austin to Boston, certified ADA service dog requiring specific housing and documentation handling; (3) Jonathan Marsh, San Francisco to Boston, CTO expecting white-glove executive treatment; (4) Leah Kwon; (5) Daniel Okafor; (6) Sofia Reyes. Also find Mina's escalation email to Chloe and David flagging that the existing April BrightLoop batch is not clean: Simone Richter was promised a 1BR but placed in a studio (unanswered since April 9), and Marcus Webb's Honda Civic is stuck in Indianapolis with Road Runner's April 11 delay notice unactioned.
OE 2: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "BrightLoop" or "Simone Richter" or "Marcus Webb" or "Oliver Bancroft" (or similar) to find internal flagging and ops discussion about the BrightLoop expansion. Expected findings: Mina's #customer-engagement message (C002, ts 1776999900) escalating to Chloe and David, listing the two unresolved April failures and flagging that Tessa's new batch includes non-standard moves (UK transfer, ADA service animal, executive white-glove). Chloe's ops response (ts 1777001700) ordering immediate cleanup of Marcus Webb and Simone Richter, warning not to recycle the Vectral ESA dog precedent for Priya Naidu's ADA case, and noting that Oliver Bancroft's London-to-Boston move is not a domestic relocation and requires international partner infrastructure MoveOps does not have.
OE 3: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "pricing" or "BrightLoop" or "Alejandro" or "tier" (or similar) to find Alejandro's pricing gap analysis. Expected finding: Alejandro's message (ts 1777025400) breaking down which BrightLoop moves fit existing pricing models and which do not. Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, and Sofia Reyes can fit a standard domestic tier. Oliver Bancroft (London to Boston) requires immigration timing risk assessment, customs coordination, and partner pass-through costs that were never built into system pricing. Jonathan Marsh is only partially covered higher-touch sourcing and tighter response SLAs were never formalized as a white-glove tier. No international pricing tier exists in the current framework.
OE 4: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "capacity" or "coordinator" or "May" (or similar), or retrieve conversation history of channel C004 (#executive) using conversations_history, to find the ops capacity assessment. Expected findings: Chloe's #executive message (C004, ts 1777121700, April 25) flagging that coordinator load is at max before BrightLoop is even kicked off, noting BrightLoop is actually eight workstreams (six new plus two unresolved from April), asking what happened to the Terraform coordinator hire Elena mentioned. David's #executive message (C004, ts 1777122780, April 25) requesting one temporary coordinator for 4-6 weeks. Chloe's #operations follow-up confirming no answer from Elena on temp coverage and planning as if no extra coordinator before May 1.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Meridian" or "UK relocation" or "Asha Patel" or "London" (or similar) to find international partner options for Oliver Bancroft's move. Expected findings: Asha Patel's January cold email from Meridian Global Mobility (sender: partnerships@meridianglobalmobility.co.uk) offering UK-to-US relocation partnership services including customs coordination, household goods shipping, temporary housing, and immigration-partner introductions. David's January forward to Mina with the note "maybe worth keeping in the back pocket if a client ever asks for a London move." David's reactivation email to Meridian (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, CC: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com) resurfacing Meridian's January note and requesting a call to discuss Oliver Bancroft's London-to-Boston case, acknowledging this is MoveOps' first UK-origin relocation.
OE 6: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Simone Richter" or "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or "UrbanNest" (or similar) to find the specific unresolved April batch issues. Expected findings: Simone Richter's complaint that her apartment is a studio rather than the promised 1BR. Mina's April 9 reply promising to follow up by end of week but never closing the loop. Road Runner's April 11 delay notice about Marcus Webb's 2019 Honda Civic with a revised delivery window. Marcus Webb's follow-up emails that went unanswered. Chloe's ops gaps email listing both issues and ordering immediate ownership assignment.
OE 7: Search Airtable using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table Relocations (tblRelocations01) with field Company and value "BrightLoop" (or similar) to find existing BrightLoop relocation records and confirm no records exist yet for the six new employees. Expected findings: existing records for Jordan Ekwueme (Completed, San Diego to Boston, Suki Patel), Jordan Whitfield (In Progress, Minneapolis to Denver, Javier Morales), Preethi Nair (In Progress, Atlanta to Raleigh, Blessing Okafor), Simone Richter (In Progress, Chicago to Boston), Marcus Webb (In Progress, Atlanta to Boston). No records for Oliver Bancroft, Priya Naidu, Jonathan Marsh, Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, or Sofia Reyes.
OE 8: Search Linear using linear_list_issues with queries related to "BrightLoop" (or similar) to find existing tracking tickets. Expected findings: Issue 412 (linear_issue_c16357d188c6, "BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion") and Issue 417 (linear_issue_f85be674c9b8, "Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace"). Also older Jordan Ekwueme issues (322, 323, 324) for apartment, flight, and moving coordination.
OE 9: Search CRM using crm_search_companies with name "BrightLoop" (or similar) to find the company record and then crm_search_deals with company_id "company_brightloop" (or similar) to find existing deals. Expected findings: company record company_brightloop (BrightLoop Analytics, domain brightloopanalytics.com). Two existing deals: deal_brightloop_q2 ($7,000, stage qualifiedtobuy, Q2 2026 1-employee relocation) and deal_brightloop_apr2026 ($11,350, stage closedwon, April 2026 Simone Richter + Marcus Webb relocations). No deal exists yet for the May expansion batch.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_customers with criteria related to "BrightLoop" (or similar) to understand the financial picture. Expected findings: Outstanding invoices for BrightLoop Analytics relocations, including billing for Jordan Whitfield and Preethi Nair relocations and for the April 2026 Simone Richter + Marcus Webb relocations (approximately $11,350 based on the CRM deal value). This helps establish the current accounts receivable position before adding six more relocations to the pipeline.
OE 11: Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts with queries for "Tessa Moreno" and "David Chen" (or similar) to retrieve verified email addresses before sending communications. Expected findings: tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com and david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 12: Create a CRM deal for the BrightLoop May expansion using crm_create_deal. Required parameters: dealname (BrightLoop Analytics May 2026 Expansion 6 Relocations, or similar), dealstage (qualification or similar early stage). Optional parameters: company_id (company_brightloop), description (covering the six employees, noting non-standard requirements for Oliver Bancroft UK transfer, Priya Naidu ADA service dog, and Jonathan Marsh white-glove executive treatment), amount (to be determined pending pricing analysis).
OE 13: Create Airtable relocation records using airtable_create_record on base appMoveOpsOps001, table Relocations for each of the six new employees. Fields for each record should include Name, Company (BrightLoop Analytics), Status (Inquiry or In Progress), Origin, Destination (Boston for all six), and Special Requirements where applicable. The six records:
(1) Oliver Bancroft Origin: London, UK; Special Requirements: UK-to-US international transfer, visa support, customs/shipping coordination, temporary housing
(2) Priya Naidu Origin: Austin, TX; Special Requirements: certified ADA service dog, requires specific pet-friendly housing and ADA documentation handling
(3) Jonathan Marsh Origin: San Francisco, CA; Special Requirements: CTO, white-glove executive relocation treatment
(4) Leah Kwon Origin: to be confirmed from Tessa's details
(5) Daniel Okafor Origin: to be confirmed from Tessa's details
(6) Sofia Reyes Origin: to be confirmed from Tessa's details
OE 14: Create a Linear issue using linear_create_issue to track the BrightLoop May expansion coordination. Title: "BrightLoop Analytics May 2026 Expansion 6 Relocations Coordination" (or similar). Description should cover all six employees, flag the three non-standard moves (Oliver Bancroft international, Priya Naidu ADA, Jonathan Marsh white-glove), note the dependency on resolving Simone Richter and Marcus Webb issues from the April batch, and reference the capacity constraints flagged by Chloe and David. Team: team_operations or team_relocations. Priority: urgent or P1 given the May timeline.
OE 15: Send email using send_email from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com with a concrete plan covering the six May relocations. Content should include: honest timelines for what MoveOps can and cannot commit to the three standard domestic moves (Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, Sofia Reyes) can proceed on normal timelines; Oliver Bancroft's London-to-Boston move requires international partner coordination with a UK mobility firm and will take longer; Priya Naidu's ADA service dog housing requires specific documentation and landlord coordination; Jonathan Marsh's white-glove executive treatment is being scoped. Should also acknowledge the outstanding issues from the April batch (Simone Richter apartment, Marcus Webb vehicle) and confirm those are being resolved in parallel. Should not overpromise on capabilities MoveOps does not yet have (international relocation, formalized white-glove tier).
OE 16: Post a message in channel C002 (#customer-engagement) using conversations_add_message to update David Chen on the BrightLoop expansion status. Content should summarize: Tessa's six-employee May expansion request, the three non-standard moves and their capability gaps (no international pricing tier, no formalized white-glove service, ADA service animal handling), the unresolved April batch issues that must be fixed before the expansion proceeds, the ops capacity constraint (Chloe and David both flagged this in #executive), the Meridian Global Mobility partner option for Oliver Bancroft's UK move, the CRM deal and tracking records created, and the plan sent to Tessa.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "BrightLoop" or "Series B" or "Tessa Moreno" (or similar) to find Tessa's expansion request and Mina's prior communications. Should also acknowledge the outstanding issues from the April batch (Simone Richter apartment, Marcus Webb vehicle) and confirm those are being resolved in parallel. Should not overpromise on capabilities MoveOps does not yet have (international relocation, formalized white-glove tier).
OE 16: Post a message in channel C002 (#customer-engagement) using conversations_add_message to update David Chen on the BrightLoop expansion status. Content should summarize: Tessa's six-employee May expansion request, the three non-standard moves and their capability gaps (no international pricing tier, no formalized white-glove service, ADA service animal handling), the unresolved April batch issues that must be fixed before the expansion proceeds, the ops capacity constraint (Chloe and David both flagged this in #executive), the Meridian Global Mobility partner option for Oliver Bancroft's UK move, the CRM deal and tracking records created, and the plan sent to Tessa.

query construction

All search_emails calls used queries like 'Tessa BrightLoop', 'BrightLoop', 'Mina BrightLoop', 'Tessa six May relocations' — none targeted international partner discovery.
✓Run #2search_emails was called with query='Meridian' — a targeted query specifically aimed at finding the Meridian Global Mobility partnership email, distinct from generic BrightLoop searches. The agent also searched for 'Oliver Bancroft London Boston' as an additional targeted query.
✓Run #3The agent called search_emails with query='Meridian' and also conversations_search_messages with search_query='Meridian international partner relocation' — both directly targeting the Meridian/international partner angle, satisfying the requirement for a Meridian/UK/international-related search query.

✓Run #5The agent called search_emails with query='Meridian UK relocation partner' — a targeted query containing both 'Meridian' and 'UK' terms, distinct from generic BrightLoop searches. This satisfies the requirement for a targeted partner search query.
✓Run #6The agent called search_emails with query 'Meridian' — a targeted query distinct from generic BrightLoop searches — to find the Meridian Global Mobility partnership email. This demonstrates the agent correctly inferred a specific international partner search was needed.
